title: Fermented Aerated Energizing Mexican Birds-Eye Chillies Bake
description: A unique recipe blending the flavors of Mexico with the energy of fermented and aerated ingredients, featuring Birds-Eye chillies and a wood-fired bake, suitable for raw-friendly diets.
instructions: Combine Birds-Eye chillies, sweet potato, coconut oil, ginger, and garlic in a mason jar, leaving 1 inch of space at the top.
Cover the jar with a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh filter and secure with a rubber band.
Place the jar in a warm, dark place for 2-3 days, allowing it to ferment.
After fermentation, rinse the jar with cold water and gently agitate the mixture to aerate it.
Preheat a wood-fired oven or a conventional oven to 400°F (200°C).
Transfer the fermented mixture to a baking dish and top with cilantro, lime juice, salt, and black pepper.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the flavors are fully incorporated.
Remove from heat and let cool before serving. If using yogurt, stir it in before serving.
